purpose: Add Bayesian posterior and bounded retry-success estimates on top of existing Ananta benchmark/evidence data without duplicating benchmark runners, planning-track verification, or model-routing infrastructure.
goal: Improve model benchmark interpretation and task-success forecasting by deriving posterior success/quality probabilities from existing hub/Ollama benchmark samples and exposing advisory attempt estimates for small-model retry versus escalation decisions.

findings: Existing hub and Ollama benchmark code already records success/failure, quality pass/fail, latency, tokens, cost, roles, task kinds, and samples.
Existing benchmark scoring already computes success_rate, quality_rate, latency_score, cost_score, and suitability_score.
Existing recommendation functions already rank models by weighted suitability score.
Existing planning-track and autopilot code already cover verification, materialization, retry budgets, status updates, and hard guards.
No existing Bayes/posterior/prior/confidence probability implementation was found during targeted code search.
The implementation should therefore add Bayesian estimation and reporting as an advisory layer over existing benchmark samples.
non_goals: Do not create a new benchmark runner.
Do not replace existing benchmark result JSON formats.
Do not replace existing suitability_score in the first implementation.
Do not create a new model router.
Do not bypass existing hub-worker boundaries, autopilot hard guards, or planning-track validation.

tasks.title: Inventory benchmark data producers
acceptance_criteria: Inspect existing benchmark modules before implementing any Bayesian code: agent/hub_benchmark.py, agent/ollama_benchmark.py, agent/services/hub_benchmark_service.py, agent/services/ollama_benchmark_service.py, agent/services/benchmark_job_service.py, agent/benchmark_quality.py, agent/llm_benchmarks.py, agent/product_benchmark_suite.py, and benchmark routes.
Document which modules already persist samples, aggregate scores, run async jobs, evaluate quality, and expose recommendations.
Confirm that the new work reuses existing benchmark result stores and does not create a second benchmark runner.
Confirm whether existing samples already include success, quality_passed, latency_ms, tokens_total, cost_units, role_name, task_kind, provider/model, and parameter fields.
Add implementation notes listing reused source files and explicit non-goals.

tasks.title: Map existing benchmark result schemas to Bayesian evidence
acceptance_criteria: Map hub benchmark samples from provider/model role buckets into a common Bayesian evidence shape.
Map Ollama benchmark samples including parameter variations into the same evidence shape.
Map benchmark job summaries only as aggregate metadata, not as replacement for per-sample evidence where samples are available.
Define how missing legacy fields are represented without raising exceptions.
Include a table or documented mapping from existing sample fields to Bayesian evidence fields.

tasks.title: Document current scoring and recommendation limitations
acceptance_criteria: Document that current benchmark scoring is weighted by success_rate, quality_rate, latency_score, and cost_score.
Document that current scoring is descriptive and does not estimate posterior uncertainty or probability after repeated attempts.
Document why low sample counts can produce unstable recommendations without a prior/uncertainty model.
Document that Bayesian estimates should augment existing score output rather than replace suitability_score in the first implementation.
Identify the exact recommendation payloads that should gain optional Bayesian fields.

tasks.title: Define Bayesian evidence record extension
acceptance_criteria: Define a backward-compatible Bayesian evidence record with schema/version fields and no required database migration.
Evidence records include source, provider, model, role_name, task_kind, parameters, sample timestamp, success, quality_passed, latency_ms, tokens_total, cost_units, and deterministic_signal.
Evidence records distinguish raw LLM success from deterministic or quality-gate pass evidence.
Evidence records can be created from both hub and Ollama sample formats.
Unit tests validate evidence extraction for hub sample, Ollama sample, missing fields, and malformed sample dictionaries.

tasks.title: Define beta-binomial posterior contract
acceptance_criteria: Define a beta-binomial posterior contract using alpha_prior, beta_prior, success_count, failure_count, posterior_alpha, posterior_beta, posterior_mean, and sample_count.
Default priors are configurable but safe; the default must not produce certainty from zero evidence.
Quality-gate pass/fail can be used as the strongest binary signal when available; raw non-empty response success is a weaker fallback only when quality evidence is unavailable.
The contract includes an explanation object describing the evidence source and prior used.
Tests verify zero evidence, all success, all failure, and mixed evidence behavior.

tasks.title: Define cumulative success-after-N-attempts contract
acceptance_criteria: Define a deterministic contract for estimating probability of at least one success after N attempts from posterior per-attempt success probability.
The contract exposes assumptions: independent attempts, correlated attempts, or penalized retries.
The function supports pessimistic, neutral, and optimistic modes by applying a documented correlation or shrinkage factor.
The result includes attempts, per_attempt_probability, cumulative_probability, mode, and warning flags.
The function never returns 1.0 certainty for finite evidence.

tasks.title: Define uncertainty and low-confidence flags
acceptance_criteria: Define uncertainty flags based on sample count, posterior variance or equivalent uncertainty proxy, and conflicting evidence.
Low-confidence estimates are explicitly flagged when samples are below configured thresholds.
High-confidence labels require both sufficient samples and stable deterministic evidence.
Uncertainty fields are optional additions to benchmark payloads and do not break existing consumers.
Tests cover low sample, balanced success/failure, high sample/high success, and high sample/high failure cases.

tasks.title: Implement pure Bayesian estimator service
acceptance_criteria: Implement a focused pure service module, for example agent/services/bayesian_benchmark_estimator.py.
The service contains no network calls, no LLM calls, and no benchmark execution side effects.
The service exposes pure functions for evidence normalization, posterior calculation, uncertainty labeling, and success-after-N estimation.
The service handles malformed, empty, or legacy samples defensively.
Tests cover all exported functions without requiring Ollama, external providers, live workers, or filesystem writes except temporary fixtures.

tasks.title: Add evidence extraction adapters for hub and Ollama benchmarks
acceptance_criteria: Add adapter functions that read existing hub and Ollama benchmark samples and convert them to Bayesian evidence records.
Adapters do not alter existing persistence formats for hub_benchmark_results.json or ollama_benchmark_results.json.
Adapters preserve provider/model separation for hub benchmarks and parameter variation separation for Ollama benchmarks.
Adapters support filtering by role_name, task_kind, provider, model, and parameters where existing recommendation functions already support filters.
Tests prove adapters produce identical evidence counts to the filtered sample counts used by existing recommendation logic.

tasks.title: Integrate posterior fields into benchmark scoring output
acceptance_criteria: Extend existing benchmark score objects with optional bayesian_estimate fields while preserving current score_hub_bucket and score_ollama_bucket fields.
Bayesian fields include posterior_success_probability, posterior_quality_probability, evidence_count, success_count, failure_count, uncertainty, and estimate_status.
Existing suitability_score remains present and numerically unchanged unless a later explicitly separate task changes routing policy.
Hub benchmark rows and Ollama benchmark rows remain backward compatible for consumers that ignore unknown fields.
Tests verify old expected keys are still present and new Bayesian keys appear only when evidence can be computed.

tasks.title: Integrate attempt estimates into recommendation payloads
acceptance_criteria: Extend recommendation payloads with optional Bayesian interpretation fields without automatically changing selected models.
Payloads include estimated_attempts_for_50_percent, estimated_attempts_for_80_percent, estimated_attempts_for_95_percent, and low_confidence flag where computable.
Recommendation output explains when a cheap small model is statistically worth retrying before escalation.
Recommendation output explains when evidence supports escalation because expected attempts exceed configured limits.
Tests verify recommendation payloads for small-high-uncertainty, small-low-probability, and strong-high-probability model cases.

tasks.title: Add Bayesian benchmark tests for zero and low sample cases
acceptance_criteria: Add deterministic tests for zero evidence and low sample count handling.
Zero evidence returns the configured prior, not 0.0, 1.0, or a fabricated benchmark conclusion.
Low sample count sets low_confidence or equivalent warning status.
Tests verify no division-by-zero and no missing-key exceptions.
Tests are pure unit tests and do not call live benchmark services.

tasks.title: Add tests for posterior updates from pass/fail evidence
acceptance_criteria: Add tests for posterior updates from pass/fail evidence.
One success increases posterior mean relative to the prior when using the default prior.
One failure decreases posterior mean relative to the prior when using the default prior.
Mixed evidence produces a posterior between all-success and all-failure cases.
Quality_passed evidence is preferred over raw success when both are present and conflict.

tasks.title: Add tests for cumulative attempt probability and correlation penalty
acceptance_criteria: Add tests for cumulative success-after-N-attempt estimates.
Cumulative probability increases monotonically with N for neutral mode.
Pessimistic mode produces a probability less than or equal to neutral mode for N greater than 1.
Optimistic mode never exceeds the configured maximum cap or certainty boundary.
Tests cover N=0, N=1, N=5, N=20, and invalid negative N handling.

tasks.title: Add backward compatibility tests for existing benchmark payloads
acceptance_criteria: Add compatibility tests using representative legacy hub and Ollama benchmark payloads.
Existing recommendation tests continue to pass without requiring Bayesian fields.
Malformed samples are skipped or marked invalid according to documented behavior.
Existing benchmark job summaries remain readable after Bayesian fields are added to nested result payloads.
No existing benchmark JSON fixture needs destructive migration.

tasks.title: Expose Bayesian estimates in benchmark UI models without forcing routing
acceptance_criteria: Extend Angular benchmark dashboard models or view mapping only after backend payload fields exist.
UI displays Bayesian estimates as advisory information, not as automatic routing decisions.
UI labels low-confidence estimates clearly.
Existing dashboard benchmark panels continue to render when Bayesian fields are absent.
Frontend tests or component specs verify rendering with and without Bayesian fields.

tasks.title: Document Bayesian benchmark interpretation
acceptance_criteria: Add documentation explaining prior, evidence, posterior, and attempts-after-N in Ananta benchmark terms.
Documentation states that Bayes improves benchmark interpretation and planning estimates but does not prove code correctness.
Documentation explains that deterministic quality gates and tests are stronger evidence than LLM self-assessment.
Documentation includes a worked example where a small model has low per-attempt probability but high cumulative probability after bounded retries.
Documentation lists assumptions and caveats for correlated attempts, repeated context errors, and benchmark overfitting.

tasks.title: Document routing policy boundaries for Bayesian estimates
acceptance_criteria: Document that Bayesian estimates do not change routing automatically in this track.
Document how a future routing policy could use posterior probability, expected attempts, latency, and cost after enough evidence exists.
Document safety boundaries: no capability escalation, no tool-permission changes, and no bypassing hub policy from benchmark statistics.
Document fallback/escalation criteria in advisory terms only.
Documentation references existing benchmark recommendation paths rather than proposing a new router.

tasks.title: Add release evidence entry and migration notes
acceptance_criteria: Add a release evidence or implementation note describing files changed and compatibility expectations.
Include migration notes that existing benchmark result JSON files remain valid.
Include rollback notes: removing Bayesian display/fields must not remove benchmark sample data.
Include test evidence checklist covering unit, compatibility, and frontend tests where applicable.
Include a note that no new live benchmark run is required to use Bayesian estimates on existing samples.

tasks.title: Run final checks and verify no duplicate benchmark runner was introduced
acceptance_criteria: Run or document relevant pytest targets for Bayesian estimator, benchmark adapters, hub benchmark compatibility, Ollama benchmark compatibility, and recommendation payloads.
Run or document lint checks for touched Python files.
Run or document frontend tests if benchmark dashboard files changed.
Verify no new benchmark runner, duplicate benchmark result store, or duplicate model recommendation system was introduced.
Verify existing hub and Ollama benchmark APIs remain backward compatible for clients that ignore unknown fields.
Prepare final PR notes summarizing source analysis, Bayesian estimator behavior, compatibility impact, and remaining follow-up work.

summary_notes: This TODO is intentionally narrower than a new iterative verification model because the repository already contains benchmark, verification, planning-track, and retry infrastructure.
The central missing capability is Bayesian/posterior interpretation of existing benchmark and attempt evidence.
Bayesian fields are advisory in this track and must not automatically change routing or policy decisions.
All acceptance criteria emphasize reuse of existing benchmark samples and backward compatibility.

end_summaries.title: Definition of Done
items: Existing benchmark samples can be converted into Bayesian evidence records.
Posterior success and quality estimates are computed deterministically from existing evidence.
Benchmark summaries and recommendations expose Bayesian advisory fields without breaking existing consumers.
No duplicate benchmark runner, duplicate result store, duplicate model router, or policy bypass is introduced.
Tests cover estimator math, legacy payload compatibility, recommendation payloads, and no-evidence edge cases.

end_summaries.title: Implementation Boundaries
items: Use existing hub_benchmark and ollama_benchmark sample stores as evidence sources.
Keep existing suitability_score behavior intact unless a later explicit routing-policy task changes it.
Keep Bayesian estimator pure and side-effect free.
Keep routing decisions hub-owned and policy-governed.
